title: Jae Akura
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Storm}} {{Character |type=Sith Empire |image=[[File:JaeAkura.jpg]] |name=Jae Akura |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{DB|character|akura|Akura, Jae "Storm"}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him[[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]] |height= |mass= |hair=[[Black (disambiguation)|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=[[Brown (disambiguation)|Brown]] |skin=Tan |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] *[[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Would have liked to bag a Skywalker though, Crasher.|Jae Akura, to Tev Rimon|[[Legacy (2006) 19|''Legacy'' (2006) 19]]}} '''Jae Akura''', a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the service of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Empire]] during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]]. He flew under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of "'''Storm'''" in [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gunn Yage]]'s [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|Skull Squadron]], a [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class fighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|unit]]. In [[137 ABY]], Skull Squadron was stationed on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] when [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Cade Skywalker]] attempted to rescue the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Hosk Trey'lis]] from the [[Temple of the Sith]]. Given orders to capture Skywalker alive, Akura and the Skulls engaged the former Jedi's [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']], in the skies of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] until Skywalker managed to lose his pursuers in Coruscant's [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|undercity]]. Approximately a week later, Akura flew against the ''Mynock'' once again when a group of Skywalker's allies, including his mother, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]]—acting in disguise so she would not be recognized—piloted the ship as they attempted to rescue Skywalker from the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Temple, where he was being held captive. Just as the Skulls started to wear down the ''Mynock'', they were given orders to abort the mission by Captain Yage's father, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Rulf Yage]]. Akura was disappointed by the orders, since he would have liked to capture a Skywalker. After the apparent [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Darth Krayt, Akura and the Skulls were stationed aboard the [[Pellaeon-class Star Destroyer|''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[War Hammer]]'' as part of a [[Operation: Thunderstroke|mission]] to capture [[Empire-in-exile|Emperor-in-exile]] [[Roan Fel]] while he attempted to [[Mission to Agamar|meet]] with members of the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] on the planet [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]] to discuss a possible alliance against the Sith. The operation failed, however, and Fel was able to escape the ambush. Following the Sith Empire's [[Final Protocol|poisoning]] of the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] as part of Lord Krayt's command to eradicate the Mon Calamari [[Species/Legends|species]] for aiding the Galactic Alliance Remnant, Akura and the Skulls were part of a [[Devastation of Da Soocha|mission]] to poison the planet Da Soocha as punishment for harboring Mon Calamari refugees. During the operation, Skull Squadron was deployed to intercept all craft trying to escape the moon, and under Rauder's orders, they destroyed unarmed civilian vessels. After the return of Darth Krayt, Akura and the Skulls participated in the Sith Empire's [[attack on the Hidden Temple]] of the Jedi Order. During the battle, Captain Yage killed Darth Rauder after the Sith Lord ordered the Skulls to kill defenseless younglings. This prompted Yage to announce her defection to serve Emperor Roan Fel, and the other Skulls followed her. ==Biography== ===Pursuing Skywalker=== {{Quote|Hey, Gunner! It's that ship—the ''Mynock''—over there! You think maybe they just trashed the Sith Temple?''"
"''I've got eyes, Akura. Attack vector, Skulls. Looks like we're getting another shot.|Jae Akura and Gunn Yage|}} [[File:Skullsquadroninside.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Akura and the other members of Skull Squadron in their hangar]] A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], Jae "Storm" Akura served [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Empire]] [[137 ABY|seven years]] after the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Attack on Emperor Fel|overthrew]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] at the end of the [[Fel Empire|Empire's]] [[Sith–Imperial War|war]] with the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]]. Serving during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]], Akura flew in the [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class fighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|unit]] [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|Skull Squadron]], which was under the command of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gunn Yage]]. In 137 ABY, Akura and Skull Squadron were stationed on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] when [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Cade Skywalker]] traveled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] to rescue the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Hosk Trey'lis]], whom Skywalker had previously given over to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] for their [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] cast upon living Jedi. Under strict orders to capture Skywalker alive, Akura and the Skulls pursued Skywalker's [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[Helot-class medium space transport|''Helot''-class transport]] [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']], through the skies of Coruscant. Skywalker was able to outmaneuver the Skulls, and they lost sight of the starship in the Coruscant [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|undercity]]. Yage ordered the squadron to spread out and search the area for the ''Mynock'', displeased with the Skulls' performance. Failing to find the ''Mynock'', Akura returned to his [[Skull Squadron Hangar|squadron's hangar]] within the new [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] along with the pilots. While Yage berated the Skulls for their performance, the Captain's father, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Rulf Yage]], approached the group and stated that the loss of the ''Mynock'' was unacceptable. Defending her pilots, Captain Yage pointed out that the ship's identification hadn't been confirmed, and it was an unavoidable outcome due to the Moff's order to bring in Skywalker alive. This prompted the Moff Yage to scold his daughter for making excuses before he dismissed the squadron. Akura was astonished that the Captain had been able to survive her childhood, but she stood up for her father, noting that he had wanted a boy. Approximately a week later,[[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]] Akura and Skull Squadron were given another chance to capture the ''Mynock'', which this time was piloted by some of Skywalker's allies, who were planning to rescue the former Jedi from the [[Temple of the Sith|Sith Temple]], where he was being held captive. Skywalker's allies, along with the former Jedi's mother, Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]]—who was acting under the guise of her alter ego, Morrigan Corde, in order to hide her true identity—detonated explosive devices around the Temple. Skull Squadron was flying in the area in response to the explosions when Akura spotted the ''Mynock''. The squadron soon engaged the [[Transport/Legends|transport]], inflicting damage upon the vessel. However, just as the Skulls had the ''Mynock'' locked in their sights, an order came in from Moff Yage commanding Skull Squadron to abort their mission. The order had been arranged by Calixte to allow her to rescue her son instead. The Skulls were displeased with the directives, and Akura commented that he would have liked to have captured a Skywalker; however, the squadron obeyed the Moff's command and disengaged the ''Mynock''. ===Operation: Thunderstroke=== {{Quote|Look at 'em, Cannon…Snarky, red-tattooed gornts. I don't like 'em and I don't trust 'em.|Jae Akura, to Brodie "Cannon" Coburn|[[Legacy (2006) 44|''Legacy'' (2006) 44]]}} Later that year, following the apparent [[Battle on Had Abbadon|death of Darth Krayt]] at the hands of his personal [[Advisor/Legends|advisor]], [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]], Akura and the Skulls were stationed aboard the [[Pellaeon-class Star Destroyer|''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[War Hammer]]'' as part of [[Operation: Thunderstroke]], a mission ordered by the newly appointed [[Regent/Legends|Regent]] of the Empire, [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Morlish Veed]]. The mission's goal was to capture [[Empire-in-exile|Emperor-in-exile]] Roan Fel while he attempted to [[Mission to Agamar|meet]] with members of the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] to discuss a possible alliance against the Sith. Akura was not happy with the Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] being made up of mostly Sith, commenting to fellow pilot [[Brodie Coburn]] that he didn't didn't trust the Sith. Akura and the Skulls were placed under the command of the Sith Lady [[Darth Rauder]], who replaced their ''Predator''-class [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] with the Sith Empire's [[Sith-Imperial starfighter|latest-designed fighter]]. The Sith-Imperial fleet arrived over the planet [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]], the location of Fel's rendezvous with the Jedi, and the Skulls, along with the rest of the [[First Sith Imperial Strikeforce|strikeforce]], engaged Fel's [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], the [[Dauntless (Pellaeon-class)|''Dauntless'']], and its starfighters.[[Legacy (2006) 45|''Legacy'' (2006) 45]] Despite the heavy losses suffered by Fel's forces, the operation was ultimately a failure, with Fel managing to escape in his [[Defender One|shuttle]].[[Legacy (2006) 46|''Legacy'' (2006) 46]] ===Napdu=== {{Quote|Confirmed, Skull Leader. They have no weapons.''"
"''Then killing them should be easy.|Akura and Darth Rauder|[[Legacy (2006) 48|''Legacy'' (2006) 48]]}} [[File:AkuraNapdu-2006Legacy48.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Jae Akura during the mission to Da Soocha.]] Following the Sith Empire's [[Final Protocol|poisoning]] of the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] as part of Lord Krayt's command to eradicate the Mon Calamari [[Species/Legends|species]] for aiding the Galactic Alliance Remnant, Akura and the Skulls went with the First Sith Imperial Strikeforce, under the command of Moff Yage, to [[Napdu]], the fourth [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Da Soocha]]. The Sith suspected the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Azzim Anjiliac Atirue]], who lived on the moon, was harboring Mon Calamari refugees. Sith Lord [[Darth Azard]], who was overseeing the [[Devastation of Da Soocha|mission to Da Soocha]], ordered Yage to deploy the strike force's bombers to unleash the same toxin that poisoned Dac upon Da Soocha, before beginning the bombardment of Napdu. While the fleet assaulted the moon, Skull Squadron was deployed to intercept all craft trying to escape the moon. The squadron's Sith commander, Darth Rauder, ordered the Skulls to destroy some vessels trying to leave moon. Skull Three, [[Tev Rimon]], reported the vessels were civilian non-combatants, and Akura confirmed that the ships were unarmed. However, Rauder was unrelenting, and the Skulls destroyed the civilian vessels. After the bombardment was completed and stormtroopers had ensured there were no survivors on the planet, the Sith ordered the recall of their forces, considering the mission a great victory. ===Attack on the Hidden Temple=== {{Quote|Crasher to Skull Leader! Storm and Cannon have to withdraw!|Tev Rimon reporting Akura's trouble|[[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]]}} After Darth Krayt returned to take control of the Sith Empire, Akura and the Skulls participated in the Empire's [[attack on the Hidden Temple]] of the Jedi Order on the planet [[Taivas]]. During the battle, Akura and Coburn were in a position where they had to withdraw, however, Darth Rauder refused to allow them to and ordered they remain in position. Rauder ordered Skull Squadron to destroy a ship full of Jedi [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]], and unable to kill defenseless children, Captain Yage fired on Darth Rauder instead, killing the Sith Lord. Not willing to continue to serve under the Sith, Yage announced she was defecting to serve Emperor Roan Fel, and the other Skulls followed her.[[Legacy — War 5|''Legacy — War'' 5]] Overpowered, the combined forces of the Jedi, Alliance Remnant, and Empire-in-exile were forced to flee the battle and regroup at [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Anyway, these guys change their codes more often than Akura changes girlfriends!''"
"''Or than Crasher smashes his ship!|Tev Rimon and Jae Akura|}} [[File:Akura_piloting.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Akura pilots a ''Predator''-class fighter.]] Jae Akura was a male Human with [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. As a pilot, Akura was a loyal member of Gunn Yage's Skull Squadron,''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' flying a ''Predator''-class fighter in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]]. Akura engaged in friendly taunting with the other members of Skull Squadron, both on the ground and while flying, often making jibes directed at his fellow pilots. One of Akura's colleagues, Tev "Crasher" Rimon, once made a comment that criminals changed their ships' ID codes more than Akura changed girlfriends. After the Skulls failed to capture Skywalker, Akura was shocked at the way Moff Rulf Yage reprimanded his daughter. Akura was disappointed when Skull Squadron was ordered to abort their mission to capture the ''Mynock'', stating that he would have liked to have captured a Skywalker. Akura was also unhappy when the Skulls were sent on Operation: Thunderstroke, due to the majority of the task force being made up of Sith, whom he did not trust. ==Behind the scenes== Jae Akura was first mentioned in the introductory supplement to the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series, [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]], released in [[June]] [[2006]].[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] He made his first appearance in the fourteenth issue of the series, [[Legacy (2006) 14|''Claws of the Dragon, Part 1'']], released in [[July]] [[2007]]. Akura later appeared in the nineteenth issue, [[Legacy (2006) 19|''Claws of the Dragon, Part 6'']], released in [[February]] [[2008]], and the forty-fourth issue, [[Legacy (2006) 44|''Monster, Part 2'']], released in [[January]] [[2010]]. All the issues were written by [[John Ostrander]] and [[Jan Duursema]], the latter of whom also penciled the issues. Akura was also mentioned in the [[Legacy — War 4|fourth issue]] of the sequel to ''Legacy'' series, [[Star Wars: Legacy — War|''Star Wars: Legacy—War'']]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Jae Akura}} *[[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]] {{1st}} *[[Legacy (2006) 19|''Legacy'' (2006) 19]] *[[Legacy (2006) 44|''Legacy'' (2006) 44]] *[[Legacy (2006) 48|''Legacy'' (2006) 48]] *[[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] {{1stm}} *[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|akura|Akura, Jae "Storm"}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Jae Akura |pl=Jae Akura }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Akura, Jae}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy pilots of Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
